forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KQ3699DCCSTFXHK772WQKVMA
+- 01KQ369S9NJ6JT3Z3J9Q8B041E
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fa80
+project: acp-upgrade
+title: Adapt acp-conformance to ACP 0.11 (incl. mock Agent impls and fixtures)
+---
+## What
+
+Update `acp-conformance/` to compile and run against ACP 0.11.1.
+
+Primary files:
+- `acp-conformance/src/initialization.rs`, `sessions.rs`, `prompt_turn.rs`, `content.rs`, `tool_calls.rs`, `terminals.rs`, `slash_commands.rs`, `agent_plan.rs`, `file_system.rs` — each defines a mock `Agent` with `ext_method` / `ext_notification` impls. New required methods need fakes here.
+- `acp-conformance/src/responses.rs`, `validation.rs` — assertion helpers over response types.
+- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/*.rs` — integration tests that drive `AgentWithFixture`.
+- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/serialization.rs` — explicit serde round-trips on `Plan`, `PlanEntry`, etc.; new variants may need new test cases.
+- `acp-conformance/tests/common/mod.rs` — shared fixture loading.
+- `acp-conformance/.fixtures/llama/*.json` and `.fixtures/claude/*.json` — recorded sessions. Verify they still deserialize correctly under 0.11; if the wire format for any preserved type changed, regenerate the relevant fixture.
+
+Steps:
+1. Get `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` clean.
+2. Get unit tests passing: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --lib`.
+3. Run integration tests: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --test integration` against both llama and claude fixtures. If a fixture deserialize fails, investigate first whether the failure is a real protocol change (regenerate the fixture by re-running the recording flow) or a regression in the agent implementation.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` passes.
+- [ ] `cargo clippy -p acp-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
+- [ ] All conformance tests pass for both `llama` and `claude` fixture sets.
+- [ ] Any regenerated fixtures are committed; the diff is reviewed and the new wire format is documented in the task comments.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance` — green for both fixture sets.
+
+## Workflow
+- Adaptation + fixture refresh.
+- Treat fixture changes carefully — they're the canonical wire-format snapshot. A binary "regenerate everything" pass is wrong; we want to understand each diff.
+
+## Depends on
+- 01KQ3699DCCSTFXHK772WQKVMA (claude-agent adapted — required to regenerate claude fixtures).
+- 01KQ369S9NJ6JT3Z3J9Q8B041E (llama-agent adapted — required to regenerate llama fixtures).
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What

Update `acp-conformance/` to compile and run against ACP 0.11.1.

Primary files:
- `acp-conformance/src/initialization.rs`, `sessions.rs`, `prompt_turn.rs`, `content.rs`, `tool_calls.rs`, `terminals.rs`, `slash_commands.rs`, `agent_plan.rs`, `file_system.rs` — each defines a mock `Agent` with `ext_method` / `ext_notification` impls. New required methods need fakes here.
- `acp-conformance/src/responses.rs`, `validation.rs` — assertion helpers over response types.
- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/*.rs` — integration tests that drive `AgentWithFixture`.
- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/serialization.rs` — explicit serde round-trips on `Plan`, `PlanEntry`, etc.; new variants may need new test cases.
- `acp-conformance/tests/common/mod.rs` — shared fixture loading.
- `acp-conformance/.fixtures/llama/*.json` and `.fixtures/claude/*.json` — recorded sessions. Verify they still deserialize correctly under 0.11; if the wire format for any preserved type changed, regenerate the relevant fixture.

Steps:
1. Get `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` clean.
2. Get unit tests passing: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --lib`.
3. Run integration tests: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --test integration` against both llama and claude fixtures. If a fixture deserialize fails, investigate first whether the failure is a real protocol change (regenerate the fixture by re-running the recording flow) or a regression in the agent implementation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy -p acp-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] All conformance tests pass for both `llama` and `claude` fixture sets.
- [ ] Any regenerated fixtures are committed; the diff is reviewed and the new wire format is documented in the task comments.

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance` — green for both fixture sets.

## Workflow
- Adaptation + fixture refresh.
- Treat fixture changes carefully — they're the canonical wire-format snapshot. A binary "regenerate everything" pass is wrong; we want to understand each diff.

## Depends on
- 01KQ3699DCCSTFXHK772WQKVMA (claude-agent adapted — required to regenerate claude fixtures).
- 01KQ369S9NJ6JT3Z3J9Q8B041E (llama-agent adapted — required to regenerate llama fixtures).
Adapt acp-conformance to ACP 0.11 (incl. mock Agent impls and fixtures)

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,20 +11,32 @@
 ---
 ## What

-Update `acp-conformance/` to compile and run against ACP 0.11.1.
+**REWRITE** `acp-conformance/` against the new ACP 0.11.1 SDK design. Each scenario file contains a mock `Agent` written against the old trait surface — those mocks all need to be re-implemented in the new builder/handler pattern (see spike findings on task 01KQ367HE0Z8ZSXY90CTT8QYGG and migration guide https://agentclientprotocol.github.io/rust-sdk/migration_v0.11.x.html).

-Primary files:
-- `acp-conformance/src/initialization.rs`, `sessions.rs`, `prompt_turn.rs`, `content.rs`, `tool_calls.rs`, `terminals.rs`, `slash_commands.rs`, `agent_plan.rs`, `file_system.rs` — each defines a mock `Agent` with `ext_method` / `ext_notification` impls. New required methods need fakes here.
-- `acp-conformance/src/responses.rs`, `validation.rs` — assertion helpers over response types.
-- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/*.rs` — integration tests that drive `AgentWithFixture`.
-- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/serialization.rs` — explicit serde round-trips on `Plan`, `PlanEntry`, etc.; new variants may need new test cases.
+## Spike-confirmed scope
+
+### Mock Agent rewrites
+Every scenario file under `acp-conformance/src/` defines a mock `Agent` to drive the conformance harness. Each one rewrites:
+- `acp-conformance/src/initialization.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/sessions.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/prompt_turn.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/content.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/tool_calls.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/terminals.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/slash_commands.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/agent_plan.rs`
+- `acp-conformance/src/file_system.rs`
+
+### Helper module migration
+- `acp-conformance/src/responses.rs`, `validation.rs` — schema-type imports move from `agent_client_protocol::X` → `agent_client_protocol::schema::X`.
+
+### Tests
+- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/*.rs` — drive `AgentWithFixture` (depends on extras crate's new wrapper API).
+- `acp-conformance/tests/integration/serialization.rs` — explicit serde round-trips on `Plan`, `PlanEntry`, etc. The schema crate jumped from 0.11.4 → 0.12.0; spot-check that the wire format hasn't shifted (likely fine — `#[non_exhaustive]` enables additive changes without wire breakage).
 - `acp-conformance/tests/common/mod.rs` — shared fixture loading.
-- `acp-conformance/.fixtures/llama/*.json` and `.fixtures/claude/*.json` — recorded sessions. Verify they still deserialize correctly under 0.11; if the wire format for any preserved type changed, regenerate the relevant fixture.

-Steps:
-1. Get `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` clean.
-2. Get unit tests passing: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --lib`.
-3. Run integration tests: `cargo nextest run -p acp-conformance --test integration` against both llama and claude fixtures. If a fixture deserialize fails, investigate first whether the failure is a real protocol change (regenerate the fixture by re-running the recording flow) or a regression in the agent implementation.
+### Fixture validation
+- `.fixtures/llama/*.json` and `.fixtures/claude/*.json` — recorded ACP sessions. Per the spike, the schema-level wire format appears unchanged between schema 0.11.4 and 0.12.0, but a full deserialize replay is required to confirm. **Treat fixture changes carefully** — they're the canonical wire-format snapshot. If a fixture round-trip fails, investigate whether it's a real protocol change (regenerate by re-running the recording flow) or a regression in the agent.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `cargo check -p acp-conformance --all-targets` passes.
@@ -37,8 +49,9 @@

 ## Workflow
 - Adaptation + fixture refresh.
-- Treat fixture changes carefully — they're the canonical wire-format snapshot. A binary "regenerate everything" pass is wrong; we want to understand each diff.
+- Treat fixture changes carefully — they're the canonical wire-format snapshot. A binary "regenerate everything" pass is wrong; understand each diff.

 ## Depends on
-- 01KQ3699DCCSTFXHK772WQKVMA (claude-agent adapted — required to regenerate claude fixtures).
-- 01KQ369S9NJ6JT3Z3J9Q8B041E (llama-agent adapted — required to regenerate llama fixtures).
\ No newline at end of file

+- 01KQ369S9NJ6JT3Z3J9Q8B041E (llama-agent rewritten — required to regenerate llama fixtures).
+- Spike findings: 01KQ367HE0Z8ZSXY90CTT8QYGG.
